2603.22920.title: The EU AI Act and the Rights-based Approach to Technological Governance
2603.22920.paper_summary: The EU AI Act constitutes an important development in shaping the Union's digital regulatory architecture. The Act places fundamental rights at the heart of a risk-based governance framework. The article examines how the AI Act institutionalises a human-centric approach to AI and how the AI Act's provisions explicitly and implicitly embed the protection of rights enshrined in the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights. It argues that fundamental rights function not merely as aspirational goals, but as legal thresholds and procedural triggers across the lifecycle of an AI system. The analysis suggests that the AI Act has the potential to serve as a model for rights-preserving AI systems, while acknowledging that challenges will emerge at the level of implementation.
2603.22920.paper_summary_zh: 歐盟人工智慧法案是塑造聯盟數位監管架構的重要發展。該法案將基本權利置於基於風險的治理框架的核心。本文探討了人工智慧法案如何制度化以人為本的人工智慧方法，以及該法案的條款如何明確和隱含地嵌入保護歐盟基本權利憲章中所載的權利。文章主張，基本權利不僅僅是理想目標，而是在人工智慧系統生命周期中作為法律門檻和程序觸發器。分析表明，人工智慧法案有潛力成為保護權利的人工智慧系統的範本，同時承認在實施層面將出現挑戰。
2603.22920.author: Georgios Pavlidis et.al.
